The Churn

Detention Paths

Map Prototype

Each line is one transfer between two ICE facilities, since Jan 20, 2025. Filter the network, or select a person to see one journey through it.

Filter the network

▣ 5th Circuit
How to read
Controls

How to read this map ×

◱Each line is one transfer between two ICE facilities. The default view shows the full network of ~970,000 transfers.
▤Filter the network with the pills — by number of transfers, or by how the detention ended (deported, released, voluntary).
▨Click a country tile to isolate one person's journey. Everything else clears. Use Trace this path to watch it unfold stop by stop.
◉Hover any facility dot for its name, type, overseeing field office, and court circuit. Zoom in for facility labels.
▣5th Circuit highlights Texas, Louisiana & Mississippi — where most paths end.
⟲Reset view returns everything to the start.

Moving around the map ×

+ −Scroll or pinch to zoom in for facility-level detail, out for the whole country.
✥Drag to pan across the map.
⇧Shift-drag (or right-click drag) to tilt and rotate into a 3D view.
⊙Double-click to zoom toward a point.
⟲Reset view snaps back to the opening angle and zoom.
69.7%
of multi-transfer detention paths since Jan 2025 end in the 5th Circuit — Texas, Louisiana, and Mississippi.
78.4% of all removals · 215,783 paths
Journey · stop by stop
